data.title: Broker: per-tool rate-limiting (beyond per-run cost budget)

data.body: Explicitly deferred by spec 6.2 ('Rate-limiting is deferred -- no rate fields in the MVP schema; only the per-run cost budget is enforced'). Adds per-tool call-rate limits (e.g. max web_search calls per run / per time window) as a DoS + cost backstop beyond the existing per-run cost budget. Requires new rate fields in the schema (Tool or AgentTool) + a migration. Post-MVP. backlog.

REVIEW (agent:claude-fable-5-reviewer, cold, commit d3f16af). Reviewed: contracts.py (RunContext fields), broker.py rate-limit gate, loop.py increment + handle_request ctx, model_profiles.json, test_broker.py, test_loop.py.

Checks: ruff 'All checks passed!'; mypy 'Success: no issues found in 36 source files'; pytest tests/test_broker.py tests/test_loop.py '40 passed in 0.74s' (implementer's full run 142 passed/1 skipped/0 failed).

Per-criterion:
1. broker() public signature UNCHANGED (cap+counter ride on ctx) — PASS.
2. max_calls_per_tool=None default ⇒ no limiting; no existing test bodies modified in diff (only additive tests + a backward-compatible _ctx kwarg with None default in test_loop) — PASS.
3. Over-limit → Denied with 'rate limit' in reason ('tool call rate limit exhausted ...'); surfaced to model via existing Denied branch ('DENIED by tool broker: ...'), confirmed by test_loop_rate_limit_denies_second_call asserting 'rate limit' in the next model turn — PASS.
4. Per-tool + per-run; only Allowed increments. Increment is inside the isinstance(verdict, Allowed) branch in run_loop; Denied/ApprovalRequired branches do not touch tool_calls — PASS.
5. Config-driven: model_profiles.json has max_calls_per_tool on all 4 profiles; broker references ctx.max_calls_per_tool only; loop fallback _DEFAULT_MAX_CALLS_PER_TOOL=5 via prof.get(...) — not hard-coded — PASS.
6. ruff+mypy clean, pytest green — PASS.
7. tool_calls: dict[str,int] = Field(default_factory=dict) (per-instance, no shared-mutable bug); broker reads via ctx.tool_calls.get(...) read-only; loop mutates. test_rate_limit_denied_keeps_counts asserts dict unchanged after deny — PASS.

Adversarial:
- Off-by-one: gate is 'count >= cap'. cap=N: call#1 count0<N allowed→loop→1; ... call#N count(N-1)<N allowed→N; call#N+1 count N>=N denied. Exactly N executed, (N+1)th denied. CORRECT. test_deny_rate_limit_exhausted (cap=1, prior=1→deny) and test_allow_under_rate_limit (cap=3, prior=1→allow) confirm.
- Broker never mutates the counter — confirmed (only .get reads); loop is sole mutator. CONFIRMED.
- Order: rate-limit placed after egress block (line 309), before budget (line 316). Egress/permission/scope still win; budget check unmoved and intact. PASS.
- handle_request: int(prof.get('max_calls_per_tool', _DEFAULT_MAX_CALLS_PER_TOOL)) — all 4 profiles have the int key, fallback sane; no KeyError risk. PASS.
- model_profiles.json: pricing block untouched, all 4 profiles got the key, JSON valid (loads fine in tests). PASS.

No findings. All 7 criteria met; implementation matches the plan exactly.

VERDICT: PASS
